summary: P2 Field Encryption Testing Complete - All 4 collections (guests, users, bookings, reservations) verified at 100% encryption coverage. Auth login with hash-based email lookups working correctly. All decryption operations returning plaintext data.
features_tested: POST /api/auth/login with demo@hotel.com / demo123 - PASSED (returns access_token with correct email)
POST /api/auth/login with frontdesk@hotel.com / staff123 - PASSED (returns access_token with correct email)
GET /api/auth/me with valid token - PASSED (returns decrypted email: demo@hotel.com)
GET /api/pms/guests - PASSED (returns guest list with decrypted fields)
GET /api/pms/bookings?limit=5 - PASSED (returns booking list without errors)
critical_code_review_comments: Hash-based email lookups (build_user_email_query) working correctly with dual-read pattern
decrypt_user_doc and decrypt_booking_doc properly decrypting aes256gcm: prefixed values
FieldEncryptionService.migrate_collection correctly marks empty-PII docs as processed (_enc_version=1)
All 4 collections at 100% encryption coverage as required
context_for_next_testing_agent: P2 field encryption task complete. All collections (guests, users, bookings, reservations) are at 100% encryption coverage using aes256gcm: format. Auth system uses hash-based email lookups (_hash_email field) with plaintext fallback for dual-read compatibility. Test file created at /app/backend/tests/test_field_encryption_p2.py covers all encryption-related endpoints.

rca_of_the_issue.original_task: Complete data encryption - encrypt users and bookings collections to 100%
rca_of_the_issue.implementation: FieldEncryptionService.migrate_collection now always sets _enc_version=1 even for documents with empty PII fields. Auth system updated to use build_user_email_query for hash-based lookups.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 9 pytest tests passed. Encryption status API confirms 100% coverage for guests (1/1), users (158/158), bookings (13/13). migrate-all reports 0 unencrypted documents.
